name: Workflow Engine
summary: The native-first runtime behind the Workflow Protocol's swappable CustomWorkflowEngine provider seam (#634, webworkflows): a dependency-free interpreter of the portable SCXML-style orchestration graph (steps + transitions + guards + parallel/fork-join + completion) that threads context, tracks the current position, and emits a { flow, from, to, context, at } step-transition event. Honors TIER-1 core + TIER-2 common operators and declares its supported set (the #085 compliance posture). Ships a CustomWorkflowEngineRegistry and a reference XState adapter as an alternative impl.

scxml.pattern: SCXML state-chart transition model (states + transitions + guards + parallel + final)
scxml.adoption: The portable WorkflowGraph is a data-defined SCXML-style interchange; the native engine interprets it directly, and the XState/SCION adapters map onto it.

customEvent.pattern: An observable step-transition event as the composition seam
customEvent.adoption: Every transition emits { flow, from, to, context, at } for the Flow Progress UX and for audit/reporting.

step-transition.description: Fired on every workflow transition — the composition seam for UX + audit

workflow-complete.description: Fired when the workflow reaches a final step

nativeFirstDefault.description: A dependency-free interpreter is the default engine; libraries are opt-in adapters
nativeFirstDefault.rationale: Native-first default — most workflows never need a library; XState/SCION plug in behind the same CustomWorkflowEngine contract when an app wants them.

portableGraphIsTheLock.description: The data-defined SCXML-style WorkflowGraph is the only lock; the engine is swappable behind it
portableGraphIsTheLock.rationale: #634 / Workflow Protocol: the transition graph is portable, so step-intents compose into a flow unaware of which engine runs it.

tieredOperators.description: TIER-1 core (mandatory) + TIER-2 common (optional); engines declare their supported set
tieredOperators.rationale: The #085 compliance-matrix posture keeps the operator set an open meta-schema — an engine advertises what it honors rather than all-or-nothing.

transitionEventSeam.description: Every move emits { flow, from, to, context, at }
transitionEventSeam.rationale: The UX (Flow Progress intent) and audit/reporting subscribe to one observable seam rather than polling engine internals.

durableExecutionIsBackend.description: History/resume, retry, compensation/saga are engine-backend concerns, not the contract
durableExecutionIsBackend.rationale: TIER-3 per the protocol: these are durable-execution backends behind the provider, never the portable graph contract.
